task:
Implement the following plan: # Add Read-Only Context to Edit Exception Modal ## Context The "Edit Exception" modal (`/vulnerabilities/exceptions` page) currently only shows two editable fields — Expiration Date and Justification/Reason. When a user clicks the edit (pencil) icon on an exception row, the modal provides zero context about **what** they're editing: no exception type badge, no target CVE/IP/asset, no affected count, no creator info. This makes it easy to edit the wrong exception or lose track of what an exception covers. The exception data is already fully available in the `exception` prop passed to `VulnerabilityExceptionForm` — it just isn't rendered in edit mode. ## Changes ### 1. Update TypeScript interface to include missing fields **File**: `src/frontend/src/services/vulnerabilityManagementService.ts` Add `affectedVulnerabilityCount` to the `VulnerabilityException` interface (the backend already returns it — the table already uses it via `exception.affectedVulnerabilityCount`, it's just not declared in the type): ```typescript export interface VulnerabilityException { // ... existing fields ... affectedVulnerabilityCount?: number | null; } ``` ### 2. Add read-only context section to edit mode in the form **File**: `src/frontend/src/components/VulnerabilityExceptionForm.tsx` Insert a read-only context block **above** the Expiration Date field, rendered only when `isEditMode` is true. This block shows: - **Type** — colored badge matching the table (IP/PRODUCT/ASSET/CVE) - **Target** — the targetValue (CVE ID, IP address, product, or asset name + ID) - **Asset** — assetName if present (for ASSET and CVE-scoped exceptions) - **Affected** — affectedVulnerabilityCount badge (if available) - **Created By** — username Use a `
` (description list) with Bootstrap grid classes for clean alignment. All fields are read-only text/badges — no inputs. Insert after line 311 (`` closing the error alert) and before line 313 (`{/* Exce
